MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-28-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF JULIE LEE'S MOTION TO REOPEN CASE IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FAILS TO COMPLY WITH CCP 473. THE MOTION IS UNTIMELY AND PLAINTIFF DOES NOT DEMONSTRATE MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. THE COURT NOTES THAT A DISMISSAL DOES NOT NECESSARILY PREVENT A PARTY FROM SEEKING TO ENFORCE A CCP 664.6 SETTLEMENT. THE LEGISLATURE AMENDED CCP 664.6 IN 1993 AND A PARTY CAN NOW SEEK TO ENFORCE A CCP 664.6 SETTLEMENT DESPITE THE EXISTENCE OF A DISMISSAL. SEE WACKEEN V. MALIS, (2002) 97 CAL.APP.4TH 429, 439. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER WHICH TRACTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302)

calendar.matters:
Mtn To Reopen Case
Off Calendar Oct-28-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF JULIE LEE's Motion To Reopen Case is denied. Plaintiff's motion fails to comply with CCP 473. The motion is untimely and plaintiff does not demonstrate mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect. The court notes that a dismissal does not necessarily prevent a party from seeking to enforce a CCP 664.6 settlement. The Legislature amended CCP 664.6 in 1993 and a party can now seek to enforce a CCP 664.6 settlement despite the existence of a dismissal. See Wackeen v. Malis, (2002) 97 Cal.App.4th 429, 439. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order which tracts the tentative ruling. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-20-2013 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Proceedings not reported. (610)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-25-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT STEVE LY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Robert M. Sellards, Esq. of Stuhr & Miller LLP (415) 585-2122 appearing for Defendant and moving party. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer overruled for uncertainty; however, demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend to add Lu as a party. CCP section 389(a). Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
